headlines.summary: Premier Li Qiang led a State Council session on energy security as China confronts the impact of the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Xi Jinping called for the strait to remain open in his first public statement on the blocked waterway.

headlines.headline: Russia-North Korea Open First Land Bridge Between Countries
headlines.summary: Russia and North Korea held a ceremony for a new 5-kilometer bridge linking the countries by land for the first time. The bridge for cars and trucks marks a deepening of ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Estonia Rejects Ukraine Warnings of Russian Threat to Baltics
headlines.summary: Estonian parliament Foreign Affairs Committee chairman Marko Mihkelson pushed back against Ukrainian warnings that the Baltics could be Russias next target, saying such narratives do not help Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Fires Rockets at Israeli Troops in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli military said Hezbollah fired rockets toward its troops in a blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement, as the US-Iran truce deadline nears. Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire multiple times.

headlines.headline: Russia Destroys Mass Graves Near Mariupol, Satellite Images Suggest
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery indicates Russia has destroyed mass graves near Mariupol used to bury residents who died during the 2022 siege. Ukraines Center for the Study of Occupation called it evidence of attempted cover-up.

headlines.headline: US and Allies Launch Major Combat Drills in Philippines as Japan Joins for First Time
headlines.summary: Over 17,000 US and Filipino troops began the annual Balikatan exercises, with Japan's Self-Defense Forces participating fully for the first time. The drills include live-fire exercises near the South China Sea and Taiwan Strait, signaling strengthened deterrence against Chinese aggression despite US focus on the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Ends Decades-Long Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Major Policy Shift
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's Cabinet approved scrapping Japan's postwar ban on lethal weapons exports, allowing sales of warships and missiles to 17 partner countries. The historic shift aims to bolster Japan's defense industry amid regional security concerns, drawing criticism from China.

headlines.headline: Trump's Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Senate Grilling on Independence and Wealth
headlines.summary: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee, pledging to run an independent Federal Reserve while facing intense Democratic scrutiny over his $131-209 million in assets and ties to hedge fund billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller. Elizabeth Warren called him 'uniquely ill-suited' for the role.

headlines.headline: US Army Tests New Arctic Vehicles and Drones in Alaska to Counter Russia and China
headlines.summary: CNN gained exclusive access to the 11th Airborne 'Arctic Angels' in northern Alaska as they test next-generation cold-weather vehicles and combat drones. The preparations reflect growing US concern over Russian and Chinese activity in the increasingly strategic Arctic region.

headlines.headline: NATO Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Bombers Over Baltic Sea
headlines.summary: French Rafale fighters deployed from Lithuania intercepted Russian bombers and fighter jets flying over the Baltic Sea, marking another escalation in NATO-Russia tensions.

headlines.headline: Dutch Intelligence Warns Europe Must Focus on Own Security
headlines.summary: The Dutch MIVD intelligence agency warned that Europe must take greater responsibility for its own security, citing pressure on Western alliances and China's support for Russia's war in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Marine Fuel Industry Scrambles for Oil Amid Iran War Disruptions
headlines.summary: Marine fuel providers face tightening supply of heavy sweet crude as the Iran war disrupts Middle East imports, with refiners competing for the same limited supplies.

headlines.headline: EU expands Iran sanctions to include Hormuz blockade officials
headlines.summary: EU countries agreed to widen sanctions against Iran to target those responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz, according to EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas. The move escalates European pressure on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: US confirms no missile defense system moved from South Korea amid Iran war
headlines.summary: A US general denied reports that a key missile defense system was relocated from South Korea to the Middle East for the Iran conflict, amid regional concerns about security gaps in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Russia intensifies coastal mining in Crimea to prevent Ukrainian landings
headlines.summary: Russian forces are mining beaches in occupied Crimea to prevent Ukrainian amphibious operations, according to Ukraine Southern Defense Forces spokesperson. "The beaches will now become minefields," said Vladyslav Voloshyn.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah violates ceasefire with rocket attacks on Israeli troops in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The IDF accused Hezbollah of breaching the ceasefire by launching rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon and sending a drone toward Israel, raising concerns about escalation despite the truce.

headlines.headline: Maersk warns ships to avoid Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire
headlines.summary: Danish shipping giant Maersk advised vessels to continue avoiding the Strait of Hormuz despite a temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran, citing ongoing volatility and lack of clarity on safe passage.

headlines.headline: Iran Yet to Confirm Attendance at Last-Ditch Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Host nation Pakistan says there is still no confirmation that Iran will attend negotiations with the United States, with just one day left on the ceasefire. U.S. forces have boarded an Iranian oil tanker at sea.

headlines.headline: Trump Claims New Iran Deal Will Be Better Than 2015 Nuclear Agreement
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said he believes the nuclear deal currently being negotiated with Iran will be better than the 2015 international agreement that curbed Tehran's nuclear program.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Seeks Billions for Drones and Air Defenses in 2027 Budget After Iran War
headlines.summary: U.S. military officials are calling for tens of billions of dollars in the next budget year for drones and air defense systems used extensively during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Chinese Student Charged With Illegally Photographing U.S. Military Aircraft
headlines.summary: A college student from China has been charged with illegally taking photos of U.S. military planes in Nebraska during a multistate road trip, raising espionage concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigeria charges six with treason over alleged coup plot to overthrow Tinubu
headlines.summary: Nigeria filed 13 criminal charges against six former security officials accused of plotting the violent overthrow of President Bola Tinubu, marking a serious political crisis in Africa's most populous nation.

headlines.headline: NATO criticises Russian and Chinese nuclear stances, urges cooperation with US
headlines.summary: NATO called out Russian and Chinese nuclear arms policies and urged both countries to work with the United States on stability and transparency at an upcoming international conference amid rising nuclear tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump's China policy drifts as tariffs stall amid Iran war focus
headlines.summary: President Trump's vow to use tariffs to reset relations with China has stalled as the administration focuses on the Iran conflict, leaving US-China policy in limbo.

headlines.headline: Canadian military pushes Arctic sovereignty as Trump threatens annexation
headlines.summary: Trump's threats to make Canada the 51st US state have pushed Canada to rethink defense relations, with a focus on demonstrating Arctic sovereignty through independent military operations.

headlines.headline: RTX raises profit forecast on strong missile demand amid global tensions
headlines.summary: Defense contractor RTX lifted its 2026 profit and revenue forecasts, citing sustained demand for missile systems and weapons as geopolitical tensions drive defense spending worldwide.

headlines.headline: US Senators Call to Halt Lebanon Army Aid Over Failed Hezbollah Disarmament
headlines.summary: The chairmen of two powerful Senate committees are calling for Congress to halt US aid to the Lebanese army over its failure to disarm Hezbollah, as the Iran conflict continues to reshape regional politics.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Firm Speeds Up Drone Development Amid China Pressure
headlines.summary: A Taiwanese defense company is accelerating drone production as Taipei bolsters air defense systems and places increased emphasis on unmanned aerial vehicles to counter Chinese military threats.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Warns Sanctions Relief Fueling Russia War Ambitions
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that sanctions relief is fueling Russian war ambitions as Ukraine steps up oil strikes against Russian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Election: What Radevs Landslide Means for Ukraine Support
headlines.summary: The landslide victory of ex-President Rumen Radevs party in Bulgarias April 19 parliamentary election signals a cooling of Ukraine support rather than a full reversal, analysts say.

headlines.headline: Trump Invokes Defense Production Act for Oil, Coal and Energy Infrastructure
headlines.summary: President Trump invoked wartime authority under the Defense Production Act to push for more oil, gas and coal production as energy markets remain volatile amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Warns "Lots of Bombs" If Iran Ceasefire Expires
headlines.summary: President Trump told PBS News that if the Iran ceasefire expires, "lots of bombs start going off." The warning comes as a US delegation prepares for talks in Islamabad, with the ceasefire set to end April 22.

headlines.headline: North Korea Fires Multiple Ballistic Missiles Into Sea
headlines.summary: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles from Sinpo, flying about 140km toward eastern waters. The launches came days after the UN nuclear watchdog warned of "very serious" advances in Pyongyang's nuclear weapons program.

headlines.headline: No Agreement After 21 Hours of US-Iran Talks, Vance Says
headlines.summary: US Vice President JD Vance said there was no agreement after 21 hours of talks with Iran, with significant differences remaining on key topics. Iran's foreign ministry said there was no expectation of a deal in just one meeting.

headlines.headline: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Half-Marathon World Record
headlines.summary: A humanoid robot from Chinese smartphone maker Honor completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record of 57 minutes. The race was held alongside a human half-marathon in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Four Contenders Vie to Lead Embattled UN as Global Crises Mount
headlines.summary: Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan, and Senegal's Macky Sall face hearings by 193 member states to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN Secretary-General. The organization faces unprecedented global instability, wars, and a budget crisis.

headlines.headline: US Labor Secretary Resigns After Abuse of Power Allegations
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has left Trump's Cabinet following multiple allegations of abusing her position, marking another high-profile departure from the administration.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Mass Shooting Reignites Debate on Gun Laws
headlines.summary: A deadly shooting in Kyiv has fueled calls to loosen restrictions on handgun ownership for self-defense in Ukraine, a country already scarred by years of war with Russia.

headlines.headline: Niger and Mali Accuse Neighbors of Sponsoring Terrorism at Senegal Forum
headlines.summary: At a security forum in Senegal, representatives from Niger and Mali alleged that neighboring countries are sponsoring terrorism, highlighting ongoing regional tensions after their break with ECOWAS.

headlines.headline: Italy's Meloni to Amend Controversial Migrant Repatriation Bonus
headlines.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni announced her government is prepared to make changes to a disputed migrant repatriation bonus scheme that has drawn criticism.

headlines.headline: US Forces Board Sanctioned Oil Tanker in Indian Ocean
headlines.summary: The Pentagon confirmed US forces boarded an oil tanker previously sanctioned for smuggling Iranian crude oil in the Indian Ocean, as tensions continue with Tehran ahead of the ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: US seizes Iranian container ship Touska in international waters
headlines.summary: US forces boarded and seized the Iranian-flagged container ship Touska, which was reportedly carrying dual-use equipment. Iran condemned the seizure as a ceasefire violation as the truce deadline nears.

headlines.headline: Hungary opposition leader pledges to arrest Netanyahu if elected PM
headlines.summary: Peter Magyar, leader of Hungary opposition, says he will respect ICC obligations by detaining the Israeli leader and reverse Orbans decision to quit the ICC.

headlines.headline: Worlds largest condom maker to raise prices 30% due to Iran war shipping disruption
headlines.summary: Malaysian condom manufacturer Karex announced price hikes of up to 30% as freight costs surge and shipping delays hit supplies due to the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping offers to work with Africa on Middle East conflict spillover
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping said China is willing to cooperate with African nations to address the economic and humanitarian impacts of the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: UK announces measures to delink electricity prices from global gas amid Mideast war
headlines.summary: Britain unveiled reforms aimed at weakening the connection between global gas prices and electricity costs, as Middle East conflict drives energy market volatility.

headlines.headline: US Seizes Iranian Tanker in International Waters as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: The US military boarded and seized a tanker linked to Iran in international waters, enforcing a blockade as time runs out on the ceasefire and last-ditch peace talks remain uncertain.

headlines.headline: Canada Vows to Defend Arctic Alone as Carney Charts New Sovereignty Course
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney pledged Canada will protect the Arctic without outside help, unveiling a military operation to demonstrate sovereignty amid Trump's annexation threats.

headlines.headline: NATO Criticizes Russian and Chinese Nuclear Policies
headlines.summary: NATO urged Russia and China to work with the United States on nuclear arms transparency and stability at an upcoming international conference, criticizing their current nuclear stances.

headlines.headline: Japan Overhauls Defense Export Rules, Opens Global Arms Market
headlines.summary: Japan announced its biggest defense export rule changes in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and enabling exports of warships, missiles, and other weapons.

headlines.headline: Trump China Policy Drifts as Tariffs Stall
headlines.summary: President Trump's tariff strategy toward China has stalled, with trade policy lacking direction as the administration struggles to reset relations.

headlines.headline: EU Seeks US Jet Fuel and SAF to Counter Iran War Strain
headlines.summary: The European Commission announced guidance on airport slots, fuel supplies, and passenger rights as jet fuel shortages loom due to the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Vance to Depart for Iran Peace Talks in Pakistan
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to travel to Islamabad for potential Iran peace negotiations as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: US Navy Minesweepers Head Toward Middle East as Hormuz Clearance Operation Shapes Up
headlines.summary: Avenger-class mine hunters USS Chief and USS Pioneer have been tracked sailing westward from Japan as Trump announces an operation to clear Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Belarus Building Roads and Artillery Positions Near Ukraine Border, Zelensky Warns
headlines.summary: President Zelensky says Russia may try to draw Belarus deeper into the war as new military infrastructure appears along Ukraine's northern frontier.

headlines.headline: US Army Deploys Low-Cost Ukraine-Tested Drones to Counter Iranian Shahed Threat
headlines.summary: Merops interceptor drones, priced at ,000 per unit, offer a cost-effective defense against Iranian Shahed drones costing ,000-,000. The Army has already purchased 13,000 units.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Reaches Out to US Automakers for Weapons Production as Wars Drain Stocks
headlines.summary: Defense officials have contacted GM, Ford, GE Aerospace, and Oshkosh about using factory capacity for military gear as Iran and Ukraine conflicts deplete US munitions.

headlines.headline: Major Natural Gas Discovery Off Indonesia Could Triple Eni Production by 2028
headlines.summary: Eni announced a 141 billion cubic meter gas find off East Kalimantan that could boost Indonesia's energy production and reduce oil imports amid global supply concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Charges Six With Treason Over Alleged Coup Plot
headlines.summary: Nigerian authorities say they foiled a plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu and charged six people including a retired major general and a serving police inspector with terrorism and treason.

headlines.headline: South Africa Police Chief Charged in $21 Million Corruption Scandal
headlines.summary: National police commissioner Fannie Masemola faces charges over an unlawful contract, marking a major blow to law enforcement leadership in one of Africa's largest economies.

headlines.headline: Trump and Top Republicans Join Marathon Bible Reading Event
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump and conservative Christian supporters are participating in a marathon Bible reading event, highlighting the administration's outreach to evangelical voters.

headlines.headline: EU Court Rules Hungary LGBTQ Law Violates EU Law
headlines.summary: The European Court of Justice found that Hungary's legislation banning LGBTQ content for minors violates EU law, in a landmark ruling with implications for rule-of-law disputes.

headlines.headline: Bloomberg: Can China Escape the Japanification Trap?
headlines.summary: As China's economy slows, analysts debate whether Beijing can avoid the decades-long stagnation that plagued Japan, with implications for global growth.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping Calls for Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman that the strategic waterway should remain open, reflecting Beijing's difficult balancing act between Iran and Gulf states during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Issues Megaquake Advisory After 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake
headlines.summary: A powerful earthquake off northern Japan triggered tsunami alerts and a rare advisory warning of slightly elevated megaquake risk. Officials urged preparedness in 182 coastal towns.

headlines.headline: Chad Deploys 1,500 Troops to Haiti for UN Gang Suppression Force
headlines.summary: Chad will send two battalions to join the Kenya-led multinational force combating gang violence in Haiti, where armed groups control most of Port-au-Prince.

headlines.headline: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO, Hardware Chief John Ternus to Succeed Him
headlines.summary: After 15 years leading Apple, Tim Cook will become executive chairman on Sept. 1. Hardware engineering head John Ternus takes over as CEO as the company navigates the AI transition.

headlines.headline: India and South Korea Pledge to Double Trade to $50 Billion by 2030
headlines.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung met PM Modi in New Delhi to strengthen supply chains amid Iran war disruptions. Focus on shipbuilding, defense, AI, and critical minerals.

headlines.headline: Chinese Carrier Liaoning Transits Taiwan Strait for First Time Since December
headlines.summary: The Chinese aircraft carrier Liaoning sailed through the Taiwan Strait in a move that comes days after a Japanese destroyer made a similar passage on a historically sensitive date for China.

headlines.headline: IEA Chief: Iran War Creating Worst Energy Crisis in History
headlines.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency says the US-Iran conflict has created the biggest energy crisis ever, surpassing the combined effects of the 1973, 1979, and 2022 crises when combined with Russian gas supply disruptions.

headlines.headline: Russia Sees Growing Interest in Orlan Drones Amid Middle East Conflict
headlines.summary: Russian drone manufacturer STC reports increased foreign interest in Orlan-series UAVs at the DSA exhibition in Malaysia, citing the Middle East conflict and demand for cost-effective surveillance systems.

headlines.headline: Palantir Faces Backlash Over AI-Driven Military Doctrine
headlines.summary: The defense tech company sparked debate with a social media thread promoting AI-driven military deterrence, arguing Silicon Valley has an obligation to support national defense and that AI will define the next era of warfare.

headlines.headline: Oil Markets on Edge as Trump Signals No Ceasefire Extension Without Deal
headlines.summary: President Trump told Bloomberg the US-Iran ceasefire is highly unlikely to be extended without an agreement, adding the naval blockade outside the Strait of Hormuz will remain until a deal is signed.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Junta Leader Offers Peace Talks, Rebel Groups Reject
headlines.summary: Myanmar's new military-backed president Min Aung Hlaing proposed peace talks with opposition groups within 100 days, but the Karen National Union and Chin National Front rejected the offer, saying they have no plans to negotiate with an illegitimate administration.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Visits Cameroon to Address Separatist Conflict
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon for a four-nation Africa tour, with separatists declaring a three-day ceasefire to allow safe passage. The visit highlights one of the world's most neglected conflicts in the country's Anglophone regions, where thousands have died since 2017.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Opposition Leader Machado Plans Return, Urges Elections
headlines.summary: Nobel Peace Prize winner Maria Corina Machado said she expects to return to Venezuela by end of 2026 and is pressing for swift elections. She warned that delays risk civil unrest as Venezuelans expect major changes after Maduro's capture.

headlines.headline: Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Defense Shift
headlines.summary: Japan has overturned decades of postwar pacifist policy by lifting restrictions on overseas arms sales, opening the door for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons as it seeks to counter regional security threats.

headlines.headline: UN Secretary-General Candidates Face Live Public Grillings in New York
headlines.summary: Four candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general face televised hearings Tuesday and Wednesday, marking a rare public scrutiny process for the leadership of the crisis-wracked global organization.

headlines.headline: US Pushes for Iran Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Security remains tight in Pakistan ahead of hoped-for talks between US and Iranian delegations as a two-week ceasefire set to expire Wednesday, with Vance and top officials expected to arrive in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: Japan Overhauls Pacifist Defense Policy to Enter Global Arms Market
headlines.summary: The Japanese government has scrapped restrictions on overseas arms sales in a major shift from post-WWII pacifist policies, seeking to counter regional security threats from China and North Korea.

headlines.headline: Iran Demands Release of Seized Vessel as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to depart for Islamabad ahead of potential peace talks, while Iran files UN complaint over US seizure of cargo vessel Touska.

headlines.headline: Iran grants field commanders greater autonomy over Iraq militias amid war pressures
headlines.summary: Iran has given its commanders more power to direct militia operations in Iraq without central approval, a decentralization driven by the ongoing conflict with the US. This shift could lead to more unpredictable attacks and complicates diplomatic efforts.

headlines.headline: Seized Iranian ship Touska carried dual-use equipment, US sources say
headlines.summary: The Iranian-flagged container ship seized by US forces on Sunday was likely carrying equipment that could have both civilian and military applications. The seizure highlights continued enforcement of the US naval blockade despite ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Board of Peace in talks with DP World on Gaza reconstruction
headlines.summary: Representatives of Trump's "Board of Peace" have held discussions with Dubai-owned DP World about rebuilding Gaza infrastructure. The talks signal early planning for post-war reconstruction even as ceasefire negotiations continue.

headlines.headline: Weak Indian monsoon threatens volatile crop prices amid Iran crisis
headlines.summary: A forecasted weak monsoon in India could drive agricultural price volatility at a time when global food markets are already stressed by the Iran conflict and Hormuz disruptions. The combination could hit Asian consumers particularly hard.

headlines.headline: US military veterans arrested on Capitol Hill protesting Iran war
headlines.summary: Dozens of US military veterans were arrested while protesting the Iran war at the Capitol. Anti-war groups say the conflict is deeply unpopular and becoming a political crisis for the Trump administration as ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: Trump China policy drifts as tariffs stall amid Iran war focus
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump vowed to reset relations with China through tariffs, but the Iran war has shifted priorities, leaving his China policy without clear direction.

headlines.headline: Pakistan confident Iran will attend US peace talks
headlines.summary: A senior Pakistani official told Reuters that Pakistan is confident it can get Iran to attend talks with the United States, as diplomatic efforts intensify before the ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: Vice President Vance has not yet departed for Iran talks in Pakistan
headlines.summary: JD Vance remains in the US as prospects for a second round of negotiations with Iran remain unclear. Iran has condemned recent US naval actions as armed piracy.

headlines.headline: Board of Peace envoy says Gaza disarmament plan needs quick progress
headlines.summary: The lead envoy for Trumps Board of Peace expressed cautious optimism about a Gaza disarmament plan, but cautioned Hamas talks will not be easy and time is critical.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall as markets bet on US-Iran peace talks proceeding
headlines.summary: Crude oil fell over $1 per barrel on expectations that US-Iran peace talks will take place this week, potentially reopening critical Middle East supply routes.

headlines.headline: Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer resigns from Trump Cabinet amid abuse allegations
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer left President Trump's Cabinet after allegations including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She is the third Cabinet member to depart, following the firing of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and Attorney General Pam Bondi.

headlines.headline: US expresses confidence in Iran peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
headlines.summary: The United States expressed confidence that peace talks with Iran would proceed in Pakistan, with a senior Iranian official saying Tehran is considering joining. The fragile two-week ceasefire is set to expire amid uncertainty over whether negotiations will continue.

headlines.headline: China's Xi calls for Strait of Hormuz to remain open in talks with Saudi crown prince
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping held a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, calling for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained as Beijing steps up diplomatic efforts to help end the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Trump denies Israel influenced his decision to attack Iran
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump stated that Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, pushing back against reports that Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision and responding to criticism from right-wing commentators.

headlines.headline: UN secretary-general race heats up as candidates emerge to replace Guterres
headlines.summary: The 10th United Nations secretary-general will be elected this year for a term starting January 1, 2027. Multiple candidates are positioning themselves to succeed Antonio Guterres as the race gains momentum.

headlines.headline: Japan lifts decades-old ban on lethal arms exports in major policy shift
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, marking a significant shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

headlines.headline: China's youth unemployment rises to 16.9% ahead of record graduation season
headlines.summary: China's official youth jobless rate climbed to 16.9% in March, with another 12.7 million graduates set to enter the workforce, deepening the employment crunch.

headlines.headline: China bets on coal conversion to secure energy amid Iran war disruption
headlines.summary: Far from its eastern metropolises, China is doubling down on coal as a core element of its energy and industrial security strategy, seeking to reduce vulnerability to supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Three ships including Iranian vessel attempt Hormuz transits
headlines.summary: Three vessels, including one Iranian-flagged ship and two others with unclear ownership, attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions and blockade concerns.

headlines.headline: Major Indonesian natural gas discovery announced
headlines.summary: A significant natural gas find in Indonesia could unlock substantial new volumes for both domestic consumption and international export markets.

headlines.headline: US delays weapons deliveries to European allies as Iran war strains stockpiles
headlines.summary: The Pentagon has informed European countries including Baltic and Scandinavian states that previously contracted weapons deliveries will be delayed as the Iran conflict draws on US munitions stocks. The delays highlight how the war that began in February is stretching American military supplies.

headlines.headline: Rubio defends Maduro capture to wary Caribbean leaders
headlines.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Caribbean leaders that Venezuela is better off today after the Trump administration military operation to capture Nicolás Maduro. Caribbean Community bloc members raised concerns about the legality of the raid while Rubio promoted the Donroe Doctrine for US dominance in the Western Hemisphere.

headlines.headline: Oil prices jump 4% as Trump announces seizure of Iranian cargo ship
headlines.summary: Brent crude rose above $94 per barrel after President Trump said the US Navy intercepted an Iran-flagged vessel. Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz again after a temporary reopening, threatening to target any ship that approaches the waterway critical to global oil supplies.

headlines.headline: British universities paid firm to spy on pro-Palestine students
headlines.summary: An Al Jazeera investigation reveals 12 elite UK universities paid Horus Security Consultancy, run by former military intelligence officials, to monitor student protesters including pro-Palestinian activists. The firm harvested social media data and conducted counter-terror assessments on academics.

headlines.headline: Kuwait declares force majeure on oil shipments as Hormuz blockade blocks exports
headlines.summary: Kuwait declared force majeure on crude and refined product shipments after the Strait of Hormuz blockade prevented vessels from entering the Gulf, hampering its ability to meet customer commitments.

headlines.headline: South Korea president rejects claim minister leaked US intel on North Korea
headlines.summary: President Lee Jae Myung dismissed as "absurd" allegations that his minister overseeing relations with Pyongyang revealed classified US intelligence on North Korean nuclear facilities.

headlines.headline: Russia values seized gold producer stake at $1.9 billion ahead of auction
headlines.summary: The Russian property agency valued a 67.2% stake in gold producer UGC at 140.4 billion roubles ahead of a May auction to sell the seized assets.

headlines.headline: UK stocks fall as Iran ceasefire uncertainty lifts oil prices
headlines.summary: Britain's main stock indexes dropped in broad-based losses as fears the US-Iran ceasefire could collapse weighed on investor sentiment.

headlines.headline: Erdogan vows tighter gun laws after deadly Turkish school shooting
headlines.summary: Turkey will tighten gun-ownership rules and increase penalties after rare deadly school shootings sparked national outrage.

headlines.headline: Israel Establishes "Yellow Line" in South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel has created a "Yellow Line" buffer zone in southern Lebanon near the border, where its forces continue to operate despite a 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah, raising concerns about long-term territorial control.

headlines.headline: Philippines-US Launch Largest Balikatan Exercises Near Taiwan
headlines.summary: The largest-ever Balikatan military exercises began between Philippine and US forces, including maritime strike drills on an island near Taiwan, as Washington seeks to reassure Asian allies amid the Iran crisis.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Launches 5% Leasing Program for Defense Industry
headlines.summary: Ukraine named three state banks to provide preferential equipment leasing to defense manufacturers at a 5% annual rate, aiming to boost domestic arms production capacity.

headlines.headline: UAE Thwarts Iran-Linked Terror Plot, Arrests Network Members
headlines.summary: UAE authorities dismantled an Iran-linked terror network that planned sabotage operations, recruited Emirati youth, and channeled funds to suspicious foreign entities.

headlines.headline: Turkey Defense R&D to Boost Other Sectors, Official Says
headlines.summary: Turkish defense industry R&D achievements could serve as a catalyst for other sectors, according to officials highlighting the spillover benefits of military technology investments.

headlines.headline: Gulf states fear US-Iran talks may cement Tehran's control over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Gulf Arab states worry that ongoing US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, falling short of broader de-escalation they deem essential for regional stability.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall as markets bet on US-Iran peace talks proceeding
headlines.summary: Crude prices dropped on expectations that US-Iran negotiations will move forward this week, potentially allowing more oil supply to flow from the Middle East and easing global energy concerns.

headlines.headline: Israel tightens grip on south Lebanon despite ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel warned residents to stay out of a belt of territory at the Lebanese border, entrenching its control over southern Lebanon despite the ceasefire in the war with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Russia detains German woman in alleged Ukrainian-directed terror plot
headlines.summary: Russia's FSB security service detained a 57-year-old German woman with explosives, claiming it was a false-flag operation orchestrated by Ukrainian intelligence.

headlines.headline: Russia-friendly former president Rumen Radev sweeps Bulgaria election
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, a pro-Russia former fighter pilot, won a landslide victory in Bulgaria's snap parliamentary election, potentially shifting the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow.

headlines.headline: China warns US-Philippines-Japan drills risk regional trust
headlines.summary: China criticized expanded military cooperation as US and Philippines began annual exercises with Japan participation. The drills include maritime strike operations near Taiwan.

headlines.headline: US-Philippines military exercises test real-world readiness
headlines.summary: Philippine and US forces launched annual exercises with maritime strike drills near Taiwan. Manila military chief said operations would test readiness under real-world conditions.

headlines.headline: Gulf stocks fall on renewed US-Iran tensions
headlines.summary: Gulf equities declined on fears the US-Iran ceasefire could unravel after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel. Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely suspended.

headlines.headline: Gold eases as investors await clarity on US-Iran talks
headlines.summary: Gold prices fell as the dollar strengthened, with markets watching whether US-Iran peace talks will proceed this week after renewed tensions over the weekend.

headlines.headline: Britain Sidelined in Carney-Backed Defense Bank Talks
headlines.summary: The UK Treasury has expressed little enthusiasm for a Mark Carney-backed initiative to provide lower-cost loans to finance armed forces, leaving Britain on the margins as allies push for defense infrastructure investment.

headlines.headline: EU Commission Jobs Reshuffle on Hold as Chiefs Stay Put
headlines.summary: The first serious round of movement in the upper ranks since Ursula von der Leyen took office has stalled, with key commissioners remaining in their posts longer than anticipated.

headlines.headline: Canadian Tourist Killed in Shooting at Mexican Pyramid Site
headlines.summary: A Canadian woman was killed and several others injured in a shooting at the Teotihuacan pyramids, a major tourist destination near Mexico City. The attacker later died by suicide.

headlines.headline: Turkey Jails Eight Over Deadly 2024 Cable Car Accident
headlines.summary: A Turkish court sentenced eight people to prison terms for a 2024 cable car accident that killed one person and injured multiple others at a popular tourist destination in southern Turkey.

headlines.headline: Israels Independence Day Marked Amid War Clouds
headlines.summary: Cities across Israel moved ahead with Independence Day celebrations despite the fragile ceasefires with Lebanon and Iran, though some municipalities opted for scaled-down community-based events.

headlines.headline: Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Policy Shift
headlines.summary: Japan has approved ending its ban on lethal weapons exports, marking the biggest change to its postwar pacifist policy as the country seeks to build up its arms industry and deepen defense cooperation with partners.

headlines.headline: Trump Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer Resigns Amid Misconduct Investigation
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has resigned from President Trump's cabinet after allegations of abuse of power, including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She is the third woman to leave the administration since March.

headlines.headline: Cuba Confirms Talks With US, Seeks End to Energy Blockade
headlines.summary: A Cuban Foreign Ministry official confirmed a 'respectful and professional' exchange with US officials, as Cuba seeks an end to Trump's energy blockade amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: UN Says US Iran War Spending Could Have Saved 87 Million Lives
headlines.summary: The head of the UN humanitarian agency expressed frustration that the $2 billion weekly cost of the Iran conflict comes amid major cuts to aid budgets, noting the funds could have saved millions globally.

headlines.headline: Gaza Needs $71 Billion to Rebuild After Devastating War, Study Shows
headlines.summary: A new study by the EU, UN and World Bank estimates Gaza needs $71.4 billion over the next decade to recover from two years of war that devastated most of the Palestinian territory.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Deadliest Aerial Assault of 2026 on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russia attacked Ukraine with nearly 700 drones and 19 ballistic missiles, killing 16 people including a 12-year-old child. The assault struck Kyiv, Odesa, Dnipro, and energy infrastructure in the southeast.

headlines.headline: US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz after the destroyer USS Spruance disabled its propulsion. Iran warned of retaliation, saying it will not participate in peace talks under threats.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Jump Over 4% After Iranian Ship Seizure
headlines.summary: Brent crude rose to $94.20 per barrel after Trump announced the seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Markets remain volatile as Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to target approaching ships.

headlines.headline: Largest US-Philippines Military Exercise Begins as China Warns Against Playing With Fire
headlines.summary: Over 17,000 troops from US, Australia, Philippines, Japan and other nations began the Balikatan exercises. Japan sent 1,400 troops for the first time, while China warned participating countries they are escalating tensions.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria snap elections
headlines.summary: Rumen Radevs Progressive Bulgaria won 44.6% of the vote, positioning the Kremlin-friendly party to govern alone and marking a victory for pro-Russian representation in the EU.

headlines.headline: Zelensky criticizes US envoys for visiting Moscow but not Kyiv
headlines.summary: President Zelensky called it disrespectful that US envoys Witkoff and Kushner have flown to Moscow multiple times but have not yet visited Kyiv, as peace talks with Russia remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Ukraine aims to develop domestic ballistic defense system within a year
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced an ambitious timeline to develop a European anti-ballistic missile defense system with European partners, seeking to reduce reliance on US Patriot systems.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV joins tradition of pontiffs sparring with world leaders
headlines.summary: The popes veiled rebukes of Trump over the Iran war place him in a millennia-spanning tradition of pontiffs clashing with political figures from Attila the Hun to Napoleon.

headlines.headline: Japan Overhauls Defense Export Rules, Opens Door to Global Arms Market
headlines.summary: Japan scrapped decades-old restrictions on weapons exports, now allowing warships, missiles and fighter jets to be sold abroad. The major policy shift marks a departure from Japan's postwar pacifist constitution amid rising regional tensions.

headlines.headline: New Zealand PM Luxon Survives Leadership Confidence Vote
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Christopher Luxon retained the backing of his National Party lawmakers after calling a confidence vote amid sliding poll numbers months before the general election. He left without taking questions from reporters.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Fear Iran Could Cement Control of Hormuz Strait
headlines.summary: Gulf nations worry that US-Iran peace talks may only secure limited outcomes, potentially leaving Tehran with lasting leverage over the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallised regional fears.

headlines.headline: British Royals Mark Queen Elizabeth's 100th Birthday
headlines.summary: King Charles visited an exhibition dedicated to his late mother's fashion on what would have been Queen Elizabeth II's 100th birthday. The monarch would have turned 100 on Tuesday, with several royal events planned to mark the occasion.

headlines.headline: Developing Nations Seek Path Out of Permacrisis at IMF Meetings
headlines.summary: Policymakers from developing countries left IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle debt and deliver better lives for citizens struggling with food and fuel costs.

headlines.headline: Vance Has Not Yet Departed for Pakistan Iran Talks
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance remains in the US as the deadline for Iran ceasefire talks approaches. Uncertainty surrounds whether negotiations will proceed before the truce expires Wednesday.

headlines.headline: US-Philippines-Japan Start Military Drills Near Taiwan
headlines.summary: Philippine and US forces launched annual exercises with expanded Japanese participation, conducting maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan. Manila says the drills test "real-world" readiness.

headlines.headline: China Warns US-Philippines-Japan Drills Risk Regional Trust
headlines.summary: Beijing warned that expanded military cooperation between the US, Philippines, and Japan could undermine trust and deepen division in the region as the annual exercises began.

headlines.headline: European Stocks Fall on US-Iran Ceasefire Concerns
headlines.summary: European shares declined Monday as investors awaited progress on possible US-Iran talks ahead of the ceasefire expiry. Markets reassessed the durability of the two-week truce.

headlines.headline: Board of Peace Envoy: Gaza Hamas Talks Not Easy
headlines.summary: The Board of Peace lead envoy for Gaza said he was "fairly optimistic" about a Hamas disarmament plan but cautioned talks will take time. Negotiations continue amid broader regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Zelensky criticizes US envoys for skipping Kyiv en route to Moscow
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky expressed frustration that US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are expected to meet with Ukrainian officials without visiting Kyiv first, calling it "disrespectful to come to Moscow and not Kyiv" as peace negotiations with Russia remain stalled.

headlines.headline: China deploys warships for Pacific combat drills amid Japan tensions
headlines.summary: A Chinese naval task group led by a warship will sail through the Bashi Channel into the Western Pacific for combat drills, as the US, Philippines and Japan conduct major joint exercises nearby in a show of allied military cooperation.

headlines.headline: NATO fuel network strains European commercial aviation
headlines.summary: Airlines warn flights could be disrupted within weeks as Europe fuel system strains under competing military and commercial demand, with NATO priority fuel movements squeezing civilian jet fuel supplies.

headlines.headline: Europe convenes parallel Gaza summits as Board of Peace stalls
headlines.summary: European nations hosted two parallel meetings on Gaza reconstruction, seeking new avenues to work with the US as disarmament talks with Hamas make glacial progress and the strip faces an estimated $71 billion in reconstruction needs.

headlines.headline: Trump labor secretary resigns amid misconduct investigation
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned after an internal watchdog probe into misconduct allegations, including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She becomes the third cabinet official to leave the Trump administration in recent months.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill Five in Gaza as Hamas Clashes with Militia
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas fighters clashed with an Israeli-backed militia. The violence highlights the complex multi-front tensions in the enclave.

headlines.headline: European Defence Stocks Cool as Investors Reassess War Winners
headlines.summary: Investors have cooled on European defence stocks as profit-taking and stretched valuations collide with uncertainty over the future of warfare, with the Iran conflict highlighting the effectiveness of low-cost drones.

headlines.headline: Putins High-Tech Submarines Challenge NATO in Atlantic
headlines.summary: Russias advanced submarines are increasingly challenging NATO in the North Atlantic, with RAF Lossiemouth serving as the front line of defense against Russian incursions from the Arctic.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldier Seen Striking Jesus Statue in Lebanon
headlines.summary: An Israeli soldier was photographed striking a statue of Jesus in Lebanon, sparking condemnation. Prime Minister Netanyahu said he was stunned and saddened by the incident.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Business Group Urges Politics-Free Trade with China
headlines.summary: The head of a top Taiwan business group called for keeping politics out of trade and tourism exchanges with Beijing, after China unveiled new incentives for the island.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone strike kills one at Russian Black Sea port
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Tuapse port sparked a fire and killed at least one person, marking the second attack on the facility in less than a week as Ukraine continues targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: China's Xi urges open Strait of Hormuz in call with Saudi crown prince
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for maintaining normal ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz in a phone call with Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman as Beijing steps up diplomatic efforts to end the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Australian bank flags $503 million impairment hit from Middle East volatility
headlines.summary: National Australia Bank expects credit impairment charges to double as the Iran war roils global financial markets, highlighting the economic fallout spreading beyond the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Six women win 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize
headlines.summary: The first all-women cohort of Goldman Prize winners hails from Colombia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, South Korea, the UK and the US, recognizing grassroots environmental activism.

headlines.headline: Trump says he's not under pressure over Iran war deal as peace talks remain uncertain
headlines.summary: Iran has balked at President Trump's demands for a peace deal amid the ongoing Strait of Hormuz standoff, with the ceasefire deadline approaching and uncertainty about whether Tehran will join talks in Pakistan.

headlines.headline: China recalculates Taiwan strategy as US-Iran crisis dominates global attention
headlines.summary: Beijing is intensifying diplomatic pressure on Taiwan ahead of 2028 elections while simultaneously positioning itself as a global peacemaker amid the Gulf crisis. China has escalated tensions with Japan over Taiwan comments and is pushing the narrative that a Kuomintang victory would avert conflict.

headlines.headline: Veterans arrested at US Capitol during anti-war protest against Iran conflict
headlines.summary: Dozens of veterans and military family members protesting the Iran war were arrested by US Capitol Police after occupying the Capitol building. The demonstration reflects growing domestic opposition to US military involvement in the Gulf.

headlines.headline: Hamas disarmament talks stalled as peace envoy admits negotiations difficult
headlines.summary: Board of Peace envoy Nickolay Mladenov acknowledged that Hamas disarmament negotiations have stalled after the group ignored his ultimatum. He warned an agreement must be reached within two weeks at most.

headlines.headline: US and Iran signal readiness for war as Pakistan ceasefire talks remain in limbo
headlines.summary: The White House confirmed JD Vance is prepared to fly back to Islamabad for another round of talks, but Tehran has not confirmed participation. Both sides are warning they are ready for continued conflict if the ceasefire extension fails.

headlines.headline: Iran Considers Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
headlines.summary: Iran is evaluating participation in US peace talks in Pakistan following moves to end a US blockade of Iranian ports. The two-week ceasefire is set to expire, with uncertainty growing over the fate of negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Promises Better Nuclear Deal Than 2015 Agreement
headlines.summary: President Trump said the new nuclear deal being negotiated with Iran will be superior to the 2015 international agreement, as his administration pushes for a resolution before the ceasefire expires.

headlines.headline: Fitch Cuts Philippines Outlook to Negative on Energy Shock
headlines.summary: Credit ratings agency Fitch revised the Philippines outlook to negative from stable, citing medium-term growth risks from disrupted public investment and high exposure to the global energy crisis.

headlines.headline: EU Has Five Months of Jet Fuel Reserves, Dutch Government Says
headlines.summary: The Dutch government estimated the European Union has enough kerosene supply to last about five months, drawing on domestic production and strategic reserves amid global energy disruptions.

headlines.headline: Indian Rupee Falls as Markets Brace for Ceasefire Expiry
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee logged its steepest one-day fall in a week as wary investors braced for the end of the US-Iran ceasefire amid rising Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran signals no plans to join US peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
headlines.summary: Iran says it has no plans to send negotiators to Islamabad for a new round of talks with the United States, citing US violations of the ceasefire including the naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Less than 48 hours remain before the fragile ceasefire expires.

headlines.headline: EU hosts Gaza peace conference as Orbans defeat opens path for action on Israel
headlines.summary: More than 60 nations sent representatives to Brussels for talks on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. Hungarys election defeat of Israel ally Viktor Orban removes a key veto that had blocked EU sanctions on Israeli settlers and other actions.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria election in landslide
headlines.summary: Rumen Radevs Kremlin-friendly party won 44.6% of the vote in Bulgarias snap parliamentary elections, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscows orbit. The victory represents a major shift in Bulgarian politics.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations amid Iran ceasefire
headlines.summary: A separate but intertwined conflict shows signs of progress as Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations with a temporary ceasefire in place. The talks involve the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Enters Uncharted Territory as Pro-Russian Radev Wins Landslide Election
headlines.summary: Former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria won 44.7% in Bulgaria's eighth parliamentary election in five years, securing the first absolute majority since 1997. The result raises questions about Bulgaria's EU and NATO alignment as Radev pursues dialogue with Russia.

headlines.headline: Turkish FM Fidan Slams Greek Cypriots and Israel for Alliance Against Muslim Countries
headlines.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Hakan Fidan criticized the alliance between Greek Cypriots and Israel, saying it undermines regional stability. He said Turkey seeks to "extinguish conflicts" while Israel forms partnerships against Muslim nations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Criticizes US Envoys for Visiting Moscow Without Kyiv Stop
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky called it "disrespectful" that US envoys Witkoff and Kushner have flown to Moscow multiple times but have not yet visited Kyiv. He said the quickest way to end the war would be a ceasefire along current lines, but Russia is unwilling.

headlines.headline: French Conservatives Select Bruno Retailleau as 2027 Presidential Candidate
headlines.summary: Les Républicains party members selected party president Bruno Retailleau as their candidate for the 2027 French presidential election with 73.8% support. The 65-year-old described his candidacy as born from "duty" rather than ambition.

headlines.headline: Turkey to Train 200 Nigerian Special Forces Under New Defense Agreement
headlines.summary: Nigeria and Turkey signed a defense pact to help fight Nigeria's 17-year jihadist insurgency. Turkey will train 200 Nigerian special forces soldiers as part of the agreement.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Any Transfer of Enriched Uranium Stockpiles in Nuclear Talks
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated Monday that at no stage of current or previous negotiations has Iran agreed to transfer or dilute its enriched uranium stockpiles, signaling continued tension ahead of ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Radev Wins Bulgarian Election in Landslide
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria's Kremlin-friendly former president, has won Sunday's parliamentary election by a landslide, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member state closer to Moscow's orbit.

headlines.headline: China Deploys Warships for Pacific Drills as Regional Tensions Rise
headlines.summary: A Chinese naval task group will conduct combat drills in the Western Pacific, coinciding with joint US-Philippines-Japan military exercises that Beijing says risk eroding regional trust.

headlines.headline: Israel Entrenches Control Over South Lebanon, Warns Residents to Stay Out
headlines.summary: Israel told residents of south Lebanon to stay out of a border territory belt and not approach the Litani River area, cementing its grip despite a ceasefire with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: China Warns US-Philippines-Japan Military Drills Risk Undermining Regional Trust
headlines.summary: China cautioned against military cooperation that could erode trust as the US and Philippines began annual exercises with expanded Japanese participation.

headlines.headline: Philippines and US Launch Military Exercises Near Taiwan Testing Real-World Readiness
headlines.summary: Philippine and US forces began annual exercises including maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan, testing readiness under real-world conditions.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Says Druzhba Oil Pipeline to Be Restored by End of April
headlines.summary: Ukraine president said the damaged Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil to Eastern Europe will be operational again by the end of April.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Says Russian Oil Exports Cannot Be Ignored After US Sanctions Waiver
headlines.summary: The Kremlin commented on a US sanctions waiver for some Russian oil exports, saying Russia is a responsible global energy player whose volumes cannot be dismissed.

headlines.headline: Russia evacuates over 600 employees from Irans Bushehr nuclear plant
headlines.summary: Rosatom chief Likhachev confirms only 20 volunteers remain at Bushehr with 4 in Tehran as Russia completes evacuation of nuclear personnel from Iran amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump says Iran deal to be signed tonight in Islamabad
headlines.summary: President Trump announced an Iran deal would be signed tonight in Islamabad. No Iranian response has been given to Trumps claim yet.

headlines.headline: EU lawmakers urge Norway to share windfall energy profits amid Mideast tensions
headlines.summary: Calls grow for Norway to share increased energy profits as the Iran conflict and fragile ceasefire push prices higher, boosting Norways export earnings.

headlines.headline: US to delay weapons deliveries to European countries due to Iran war
headlines.summary: Some European nations have been informed that select US weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on American weapons stocks.

headlines.headline: Starlink outage hit Navy drone tests exposing Pentagon reliance on SpaceX
headlines.summary: During April 2025 Navy tests involving unmanned boats and drones, Starlink struggled to provide solid network connections, highlighting the Pentagons growing dependence on SpaceX.

headlines.headline: China Calls for Hormuz to Stay Open in Xi-Bin Salman Call
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping urged Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to maintain normal ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz, as Beijing steps up efforts to end the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks Set to Resume as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance is expected to travel to Pakistan for the second round of peace negotiations, though Iran has not confirmed its participation as the two-week ceasefire nears its end.

headlines.headline: Trump Invokes Defense Production Act to Expand US Petroleum Output
headlines.summary: President Trump signed a memorandum calling for expanded domestic petroleum production, refining and logistics capacity, citing national defense needs amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Fear Iran Talks May Cement Tehran Hormuz Control
headlines.summary: Gulf countries worry that US-Iran negotiations might only secure reopening of the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Tehran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway.

headlines.headline: Apple Names John Ternus as CEO, Tim Cook Becomes Executive Chairman
headlines.summary: Apple appointed insider John Ternus as its next CEO, with Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman in a major leadership shakeup at the worlds most valuable company.

headlines.headline: Trump Sends Mixed Signals on Iran War Path as Ceasefire Nears End
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump gave mixed messages on the path ahead for the U.S. war with Iran, with the ceasefire set to expire Wednesday and prospects for extension described as "highly unlikely."

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Ex-Fighter Pilot Rumen Radev Sweeps Bulgaria Election
headlines.summary: Former Bulgarian President Rumen Radev won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections, crushing long-dominant political forces and potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Strike on Russian Port of Tuapse Kills One
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse sparked a fire and killed at least one person, Russian officials said, the second such attack on the oil terminal in five days.

headlines.headline: BOJ Likely to Hold Rates Steady Amid Middle East Uncertainty
headlines.summary: The Bank of Japan is likely to hold off raising interest rates next week as fading prospects of a near-term end to the Middle East war keep the country's economic and price outlook highly uncertain.

headlines.headline: Hungary Election Winner Peter Magyar Names Finance and Foreign Ministers
headlines.summary: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar nominated key ministers including Andras Karman as finance minister and Anita Orban as foreign minister for his incoming government.

headlines.headline: South Korea-US tensions flare over North Korea intelligence leak
headlines.summary: Seoul's Unification Minister Chung Dong-young rejects accusations he leaked sensitive information about North Korea's nuclear facilities, straining alliance coordination on Pyongyang policy.

headlines.headline: China threatens retaliation against EU firms over cybersecurity plans
headlines.summary: Beijing warns of broad retaliation if Huawei, ZTE and other Chinese companies are penalized under proposed EU cybersecurity regulations, escalating tech tensions.

headlines.headline: UN reports 350,000 displaced and 177 children killed in Lebanon since March
headlines.summary: UN peacekeepers continue observing Israeli artillery fire in southern Lebanon as humanitarian crisis deepens with over 1 million displaced facing worsening conditions.

headlines.headline: EU to host Taliban officials for talks on deporting Afghans
headlines.summary: Taliban officials expected in Brussels for negotiations on returning Afghan migrants to their home country, marking significant diplomatic engagement with the unrecognized government.

headlines.headline: Romania plunges into political turmoil as Social Democrats move to topple PM
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces ouster after coalition partners accuse him of destroying their social base with austerity measures, deepening Europe's governance instability.

headlines.headline: Japan issues tsunami warnings after 7.7 magnitude earthquake strikes northern coast
headlines.summary: A powerful 7.7 earthquake hit off northern Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and evacuation orders. Meteorological agency warns of possible stronger aftershocks in the coming week.

headlines.headline: Canadian PM Carney says economic ties with US are weakness that must be corrected
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada must diversify trade relationships and reduce dependence on the US economy, signaling a major shift in Canadian economic policy amid tensions.

headlines.headline: New Zealand declares state of emergency as severe floods hit Wellington region
headlines.summary: Widespread flooding in Wellington has submerged vehicles, uprooted trees and triggered landslides. State of emergency declared as cleanup begins.

headlines.headline: Around 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
headlines.summary: A vessel carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, with approximately 250 people reported missing in the latest tragedy involving the persecuted minority.

headlines.headline: At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
headlines.summary: An overcrowded bus skidded off a mountain road and fell into a gorge in Indian-administered Kashmir, killing at least 21 passengers and injuring dozens more.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Ceasefire Falters, Stocks Retreat from Records
headlines.summary: Global oil prices jumped 6% on fears the US-Iran ceasefire may collapse after the US Navy seized an Iranian cargo ship. Wall Street stocks pulled back from record highs as investors assessed the geopolitical risk.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Not Under Pressure on Iran Deal as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: President Trump stated he faces no pressure to rush an Iran deal despite the ceasefire expiring Wednesday. Iran says it has no plans for the next round of negotiations, casting doubt on Islamabad peace talks.

headlines.headline: Iran Vows Retaliation After US Seizes Cargo Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
headlines.summary: Iran condemned the US Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged tanker as armed piracy and vowed retaliation. The incident has cast doubt on whether the second round of peace talks in Islamabad will proceed.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Officer Deploys 343 Days on Front Line, Highlighting Manpower Crisis
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian infantry officer spent 343 consecutive days on the front line, believed to be one of the longest continuous combat deployments. The case underscores Kyivs growing manpower challenges after years of war.

headlines.headline: Poland and France Discuss Joint Nuclear Exercises as US Commitment Wavers
headlines.summary: Polish PM Donald Tusk and French President Macron discussed joint nuclear deterrence exercises. Poland is exploring deeper defense ties with France amid concerns about US commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: Iranian president voices deep mistrust of US ahead of Islamabad peace talks
headlines.summary: President Pezeshkian says historical mistrust of the US government remains as second round of Iran-US talks prepare to kick off in Pakistan. The comments come as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: Saudi crown prince hosts Sudan army chief as .5B arms deal stalls
headlines.summary: MBS met with General Burhan in Jeddah amid reports a major Saudi-backed weapons deal faces delays. The visit highlights Saudi efforts to maintain influence in Sudans ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: US Navy warns sailors to secure social media from Iranian online threats
headlines.summary: The Navy issued security guidance to service members to protect electronic devices and accounts amid increased online threats linked to the Iran-Israel war.

headlines.headline: Pentagon deploys AI to vet China ties of university professors
headlines.summary: After watchdog criticism of understaffing, the Defense Department is turning to artificial intelligence to screen Pentagon-funded researchers for potential espionage ties to Beijing.

headlines.headline: African economies strain as Iran shuts Hormuz route again
headlines.summary: African nations face renewed cost pressures after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, disrupting vital shipping routes and threatening fuel imports across the continent.

headlines.headline: Israel Entrenches Control Over South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel warned residents to stay out of a border territory belt and not approach the Litani River area, deepening its grip on southern Lebanon despite the Hezbollah ceasefire. The move signals Israel's intent to maintain a security buffer zone indefinitely.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Fear US-Iran Talks May Cement Tehran's Grip on Hormuz
headlines.summary: Gulf Arab states worry that US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Iran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallized regional fears about Tehran's "golden" control.

headlines.headline: Philippines and US Launch Military Exercises Near Taiwan
headlines.summary: Philippine and US forces began annual military exercises featuring maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan. Manila's military chief said the drills would test readiness under "real-world conditions" amid regional tensions with China.

headlines.headline: Germany Summons Russian Ambassador Over Direct Threats
headlines.summary: Berlin summoned the Russian ambassador to condemn what it called "direct threats" against targets in Germany, escalating diplomatic tensions between the two countries. The move reflects growing friction between Russia and European nations.

headlines.headline: Board of Peace Envoy Sees Progress on Gaza Disarmament Plan
headlines.summary: The Board of Peace's lead envoy for Gaza said he was "fairly optimistic" that a plan for disarmament of Hamas and other militant groups in Gaza can be agreed, but cautioned it will take time. The comments come as over 60 nations met in Brussels to discuss Gaza stability.

headlines.headline: China's Xi Jinping Calls for Hormuz to Remain Open in Phone Call With Saudi Crown Prince
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained in a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. Xi urged an immediate ceasefire between the US and Iran and stressed that the waterway's reopening was in the 'common interests of all countries.'

headlines.headline: Iran May Join Peace Talks After Pakistan Works to End US Blockade, Official Says
headlines.summary: Iran is considering attending peace talks with the United States in Pakistan after Islamabad moved to end the US naval blockade of Iran's ports, a senior Iranian official told Reuters. The development could revive negotiations ahead of the ceasefire deadline on Wednesday.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Israel Never Talked Him Into War With Iran
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, rejecting reports that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision. The statement comes amid criticism from right-wing commentators questioning the war's origins.

headlines.headline: Israel Condemns Soldier's Desecration of Crucifix in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: A photo showing the desecration of a crucifix smashed by an Israeli soldier in a southern Lebanese Christian village drew widespread condemnation from Israeli leaders, the United States and church leaders. The incident has damaged Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

headlines.headline: Battle-Scarred Developing Nations Seek Path Out of 'Permacrisis' at IMF Meetings
headlines.summary: Developing country policymakers left the IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle high debt, reform economies and deliver better lives. The Iran war's energy disruptions have hit the world's poorest nations hardest.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Seeks Better Infrared Jammers for Combat Rescue Helicopters After Iran Mission
headlines.summary: The US Air Force is seeking better infrared countermeasures for its HH-60W combat rescue helicopters after one took multiple hits from Iranian small-arms fire during a high-profile pilot rescue mission. The aircraft currently lacks a capable advanced infrared countermeasures system, significantly increasing the risk of infrared-guided missile engagement.

headlines.headline: New Zealand Declares State of Emergency as Record Rainfall Triggers Wellington Flash Floods
headlines.summary: New Zealand declared a state of emergency for Wellington after the city received 77mm of rain in less than an hour—its heaviest rainfall on record—triggering flash floods, landslides and evacuations. A search is underway for a man at a flooded property, with the Fire Service responding to 150 calls for help overnight.

headlines.headline: Europol Hackathon Identifies 45 More Ukrainian Children Forcibly Deported to Russia
headlines.summary: An international open-source investigation coordinated by Europol uncovered information on 45 children forcibly transferred or deported by Russia from occupied areas of Ukraine. The two-day operation produced leads on transportation routes, individuals involved, and facilities where children were taken.

headlines.headline: China Threatens Retaliation Against EU Firms Over Proposed Cybersecurity Rules Targeting Huawei
headlines.summary: China warned it will hit EU firms with reciprocal measures if the bloc targets Chinese companies like Huawei and ZTE under proposed cybersecurity regulations. Beijing's commerce ministry submitted a 30-page document warning of broad retaliation if Chinese firms are designated as 'high risk suppliers' and excluded from EU markets.

headlines.headline: Norwegian F-35s Intercept Russian Il-38 Surveillance Aircraft Over Arctic
headlines.summary: Norway confirmed two F-35 fighters were scrambled from Evenes Air Station to intercept a Russian Il-38 maritime patrol aircraft approaching its airspace on April 13. The incident highlights continued Russian military activity near NATO's northern flank amid heightened tensions with the alliance.

headlines.headline: Powerful 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Northern Japan, Triggers Mega-Quake Warning
headlines.summary: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake struck off northern Japan's Sanriku coast, triggering tsunami alerts of up to 3 meters and a rare advisory warning of a 1% chance of a mega-quake in the coming week. Over 128,000 residents received evacuation advisories, with bullet trains suspended, though no major damage or nuclear abnormalities were reported.

headlines.headline: Over 60 Nations Gather in Brussels for EU-Hosted Gaza Peace Conference
headlines.summary: Representatives from over 60 nations convened in Brussels for talks with Palestinian Prime Minister Mohamed Mustafa on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. The EU aims to increase its relevance in Middle East diplomacy amid the Iran war, with renewed momentum following Hungary's election of a leader more critical of Netanyahu.

headlines.headline: Iraq's Ruling Shia Bloc Races to Choose Prime Minister as US and Iran Watch Closely
headlines.summary: Iraq's Coordination Framework must nominate a prime minister by April 26 as the country balances ties between the US and Iran amid the ongoing conflict. IRGC Quds Force chief Ismail Qaani visited Baghdad to influence the selection, with incumbent PM al-Sudani facing a challenge from pro-Iran former PM Nouri al-Maliki.

headlines.headline: China Expresses Concern Over US Seizure of Iranian Tanker Touska
headlines.summary: China said it was concerned about the seizure of the Iranian-flagged tanker Touska by US forces, noting the vessel has frequently traveled between China and Iran. The incident adds another layer of complexity to the US-Iran standoff as Beijing navigates its energy and diplomatic interests in the region.

headlines.headline: Ugandan and Congolese Troops Rescue 200 Captives From IS-Affiliated Militants
headlines.summary: Ugandan and Congolese soldiers rescued at least 200 civilians in a raid on an Islamist militant camp in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The operation targeted IS-affiliated militants who have been conducting attacks in the volatile region bordering Uganda.

headlines.headline: India and South Korea Aim to Double Trade to $50 Billion by 2030 Amid Global Uncertainty
headlines.summary: India and South Korea agreed to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030 during South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's state visit to New Delhi. Both nations seek closer ties amid unpredictable relations with the US under President Trump, signing MoUs on shipbuilding, chips, and technology cooperation.

headlines.headline: Slovenia's Prime Minister Golob Fails to Form Government, Opening Door to Right-Wing Coalition
headlines.summary: Slovenia's caretaker Prime Minister Robert Golob admitted his party cannot form a coalition government after razor-thin election results. The failure opens the door for former three-time Premier Janez Janša and his right-wing SDS party to attempt forming a government.

headlines.headline: French Prosecutors Summon Elon Musk in Criminal Probe of X Platform Over Abuse Images and Deepfakes
headlines.summary: Elon Musk was summoned by French prosecutors for questioning as part of a widening criminal investigation into X and its AI chatbot Grok. The probe focuses on allegations of child sexual abuse material, deepfakes, and data extraction on the platform, further straining US-EU relations over Big Tech regulation.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Condemns IDF Soldier Who Smashed Jesus Statue in Lebanon as Backlash Grows
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned 'in the strongest terms' an IDF reservist who smashed a statue of Jesus in a southern Lebanese village, saying he was 'stunned and saddened.' The IDF confirmed the viral photo's authenticity and launched an investigation amid backlash from US conservatives and Christian communities.

headlines.headline: Israel's Mossad and Security Agencies Dismantle Iranian Terror Network in Azerbaijan
headlines.summary: Mossad, Shin Bet, and the IDF announced they dismantled a global Iranian terror network operating in Azerbaijan that was targeting Western sites.
